payload.claim_text: The November Plan projects ending cash balances of $0 for 2022 and 2023, with 2023 balanced assuming $600 million of additional government funding or MTA actions. Annual deficits of $1.2 billion in 2024 and 2025, and a deficit of $1.6 billion in 2026, remain.
payload.subject: November Plan

payload.claim_text: MTA received $15.1 billion in federal COVID relief funds since the start of the pandemic: $4.0 billion in 2020 from CARES Act, $4.1 billion in 2021 from CRRSAA, and $7.0 billion in 2022 from ARPA.
payload.subject: MTA federal COVID relief

payload.claim_text: Ridership recovery remained steady at about 61% of the pre-pandemic level through the summer, and then moved upwards to 63% in September and 64% in October.
payload.subject: MTA ridership recovery

payload.claim_text: Operating efficiency initiatives will generate $100 million in 2023, increasing to $400 million in 2024, $408 million in 2025 and $416 million in 2026.
payload.subject: MTA operating efficiencies

payload.claim_text: MTA is proposing a 2023 fare and toll increase yielding approximately 5.5% in additional fare and toll revenue, estimated to generate $201 million in 2023, $362 million in 2024, $369 million in 2025 and $375 million in 2026.
payload.subject: 2023 fare and toll increase

payload.claim_text: Through 2026, the Plan assumes a combined $1.8 billion in additional fare and toll revenue from the projected 2023 and 2025 fare and toll increases, including an approximate 5.5% fare and toll increase in 2023, followed by a 4% fare and toll increase in 2025.
payload.subject: Biennial fare and toll increases

tool_args.label: November Plan deficits before COVID funds

payload.raw_value_text: $1.9 billion in 2022, $2.6 billion in 2023, $2.8 billion in 2024, and $3.0 billion in 2025 and 2026

tool_args.label: Remaining COVID funds at start of 2023

payload.raw_value_text: approximately $5.6 billion of COVID funds will remain

payload.event_name: November Plan presented to Board for information

payload.description: MTA Board will be updated in February on the outlook for legislative actions with respect to the assumed $600 million of additional funding.
EA released for public review with public hearings; comment period closed September 23, 2022

payload.gap_text: The 2023 budget assumes $600 million in additional government funding and/or additional MTA actions, both of which have not yet been specified.
payload.missing_information: No specific source or nature of $600 million in additional funding/MTA actions identified

payload.gap_text: New government funding does not materialize; ridership improvements can fall short of projections; economy slows or falls into recession; inflation remains stubbornly high; achieving affordable wage settlements; implementation of biennial fare increases; finding innovative operational efficiencies.

payload.description: Aims to reduce congestion in Manhattan's CBD and generate sufficient net revenue to support $15 billion for the MTA 2020-2024 Capital Program.
payload.status: EA released for public review; comment period closed September 23, 2022; awaiting FHWA determination
payload.location: Manhattan south of and inclusive of 60th Street

location_normalized.raw_text: Manhattan south of and inclusive of 60th Street
